flashcards.back: Abstraction is the process of hiding complex implementation details and showing only essential features of an object to the outside world, focusing on what an object does rather than how it does it.

flashcards.front: What is an abstract class in Java?
flashcards.back: An abstract class is a restricted class that cannot be instantiated but can be extended by other classes. It may contain abstract methods (without implementation) and concrete methods (with implementation).
An abstract method is a method declared with the 'abstract' keyword without any implementation. It has no body and must be overridden by concrete subclasses.

flashcards.front: What is an interface in Java?
flashcards.back: An interface is a reference type in Java that contains abstract methods only (prior to Java 8). From Java 8+, it can have default and static methods with implementation. It achieves 100% abstraction.
Yes, abstract classes can have constructors. Even though you cannot instantiate an abstract class directly, constructors are called when a subclass is instantiated.
No, interfaces cannot have constructors. They cannot be instantiated directly and are meant to define behavior contracts.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between abstract class and interface?
flashcards.back: Abstract class can have both abstract and concrete methods, can have constructors, and supports single inheritance. Interface (pre-Java 8) can only have abstract methods, no constructors, and supports multiple inheritance.

flashcards.front: What keyword is used to declare an abstract class?
flashcards.back: The 'abstract' keyword is used before the class keyword to declare an abstract class.
No, abstract methods cannot be private. They must be overridden by subclasses, so they cannot be private. If declared private, it would prevent inheritance.
100% abstraction is achieved using interfaces, which can have only abstract methods (or default/static methods from Java 8). All method signatures are hidden from the user.

flashcards.front: Why is abstraction used in Java?
flashcards.back: Abstraction reduces complexity by hiding implementation details, provides code reusability, enhances maintainability, allows focusing on what an object does instead of how it does it, and enables loose coupling.

flashcards.front: Can a class extend only one abstract class?
flashcards.back: Yes, Java supports single inheritance for classes. A class can extend only one abstract class but can implement multiple interfaces.

flashcards.front: What is the syntax to implement an interface?
flashcards.back: A class implements an interface using the 'implements' keyword: 'class ClassName implements InterfaceName { }'. A class can implement multiple interfaces separated by commas.

flashcards.front: Can abstract classes have static methods?
flashcards.back: Yes, abstract classes can have static methods. Static methods can be called without creating an instance of the class and are not inherited by subclasses.

flashcards.front: What happens if a subclass does not implement all abstract methods?
flashcards.back: If a subclass does not implement all abstract methods from its parent abstract class, it must also be declared as abstract. Otherwise, a compile-time error occurs.
